21.correct: Under a parliamentary system, the Prime Minister is the real (de facto) executive, while the President is the nominal head.

wrong.A: In a presidential system the President is the real executive.
wrong.C: A unitary system describes the distribution of powers, not who is the executive.
wrong.D: A federal system describes Centre-State structure, not the executive head.

21.fact: India follows a parliamentary system with the PM as the real executive.

22.correct: The phosphorus cycle does NOT have a gaseous phase - phosphorus mostly cycles through rocks, soil, water and living things.

wrong.A: The water cycle has a gaseous (vapour) phase.
wrong.B: The carbon cycle has a gaseous (CO2) phase.
wrong.C: The nitrogen cycle has a gaseous (N2) phase.

22.fact: Because it lacks a gaseous phase, phosphorus is a 'sedimentary' biogeochemical cycle.

23.correct: The Santhal dance is typically a circular or line group dance involving both men and women.

wrong.A: It is not a solo dance by women.
wrong.C: It is not a martial sword dance.
wrong.D: It is not a seated dance.

23.fact: Santhal dances accompany festivals and are performed with drums and flutes.

24.correct: The diameter of the human eyeball is about 2.3 cm (roughly 24 mm).
24.fact: The adult human eye is about 24 mm in diameter and nearly spherical.

25.correct: The lion is a classic example of a carnivore (an animal that eats other animals).
25.fact: Carnivores have adaptations such as sharp teeth and claws for hunting.

26.correct: The Musamman Burj in the Agra Fort, with its white-marble arches, is where Shah Jahan was imprisoned and reportedly gazed at the Taj Mahal.

wrong.B: The Moti Masjid is a pearl mosque, not this tower.
wrong.C: The Nagina Masjid was the ladies' mosque.
wrong.D: The Diwan-i-Khas was the hall of private audience.

26.fact: Shah Jahan spent his last years confined in the Agra Fort by his son Aurangzeb.

27.correct: Isaac Newton first explained the splitting (dispersion) of white light into colours using a prism.

wrong.B: Thomas Young is known for the double-slit (interference) experiment.
wrong.C: Huygens proposed the wave theory of light.
wrong.D: Maxwell developed the electromagnetic theory of light.

27.fact: Newton showed that white light is a mixture of colours (the visible spectrum).

28.correct: In July 1991, the Indian rupee was devalued by about 18-20% (in two steps) to address the balance-of-payments crisis.
28.fact: The 1991 devaluation was part of the broader economic reform package.

29.correct: The Export-Import (EXIM) Bank of India was established in 1982.
29.fact: EXIM Bank finances and promotes India's foreign trade.

30.correct: Gujarat is the largest salt-producing state in India.

wrong.A: Maharashtra is not the largest salt producer.
wrong.B: Tamil Nadu is a major producer but second to Gujarat.
wrong.D: Rajasthan produces salt (Sambhar Lake) but less than Gujarat.

30.fact: Gujarat's long coastline and the Rann of Kutch make it ideal for salt production.

31.correct: Copper reacts with moist carbon dioxide in air, slowly losing its shiny brown surface and developing a green layer (basic copper carbonate, or verdigris).

wrong.B: It does not develop a black layer in this reaction.

31.fact: This green patina is similar to what forms on old copper statues like the Statue of Liberty.

32.correct: National income is a flow variable - it is measured over a period of time (e.g. a year).

wrong.B: Money supply is a stock variable (measured at a point in time).
wrong.C: Share capital is a stock variable.
wrong.D: Foreign-exchange reserves are a stock variable.

32.fact: A flow is measured per unit time; a stock is measured at a moment in time.

33.correct: The steel (iron and steel) industry is mainly concentrated around the Damodar Valley because of the availability of coal (and nearby iron ore).

wrong.A: The textile industry is not concentrated there for coal.
wrong.C: The chemical industry is not the main one here.
wrong.D: The leather industry is not the focus.

33.fact: The Chota Nagpur plateau region has India's richest coal and mineral deposits.

34.correct: In May 2025, Mizoram was officially declared fully literate under the ULLAS programme.

wrong.A: Haryana was not the one declared fully literate then.
wrong.B: Rajasthan was not declared fully literate.
wrong.D: Uttar Pradesh was not declared fully literate.

34.fact: ULLAS (New India Literacy Programme) promotes adult literacy.

35.correct: Among the 'Navaratnas' (nine gems) of Chandragupta II's court, the poet Kalidasa is considered the most notable.
35.fact: Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) is associated with the legendary nine gems of his court.

36.correct: The Vernacular Press Act, 1878 was enacted to silence Indian-language newspapers that criticised the government.

wrong.B: There was no Vernacular Press Act in 1858.
wrong.C: The 1833 Charter Act dealt with administration.
wrong.D: The 1813 Charter Act dealt with the Company's monopoly.

36.fact: The Act was passed under Viceroy Lord Lytton and later repealed by Lord Ripon.

37.correct: The ministry/secretariat whose security was upgraded (with facial recognition, RFID and time-slot entry) in January 2025 is located in Maharashtra.

wrong.D: It is not in Madhya Pradesh.

37.fact: Modern access-control systems improve the physical security of government buildings.

38.correct: In 1990, the Total Fertility Rate in India - the average number of children a woman would have - was about 3.8.

wrong.B: By 2011 the TFR had fallen well below 3.8.
wrong.D: In 1960 the TFR was even higher than 3.8.

38.fact: India's TFR has since fallen to around the replacement level of 2.0-2.1.

39.correct: 'Tachi-waza' and 'Koshi-waza' are terms associated with judo (standing and hip throwing techniques).
39.fact: Judo techniques are broadly divided into throwing (nage-waza) and grappling (katame-waza).

40.correct: Only the second pairing is correct - groundwater storage discharges through springs (II). The first pairing wrongly links ice/snow storage with transpiration (I), so 'Only II'.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong; that pairing is incorrect.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since pairing I is incorrect.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since pairing II is correct.

40.fact: The water cycle includes evaporation, condensation, precipitation, infiltration and runoff.
